steps.title: Identify the y-intercept from the first point
steps.content: The point $(0, 3)$ tells us $f(0) = 3$, so the y-intercept is $b = 3$. Looking at the answer choices, only C and D have $+3$ as the constant term.

steps.title: Backsolve with choice C
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice C ($f(x) = 4x + 3$):
  • Check $(0, 3)$: $f(0) = 4(0) + 3 = 3$ ✓
  • Check $(7, 31)$: $f(7) = 4(7) + 3 = 28 + 3 = 31$ ✓
Both points satisfy this equation. That matches choice C — C is correct.
